name: functions
context: Provision and deploy Azure Function Apps with Azure CLI zip deployment and Core Tools publishing
info: Azure Functions is the serverless execution surface for event-driven or HTTP-triggered workloads. Agents use this skill to create Function Apps backed by a storage account, deploy zip packages through Kudu, optionally publish directly with Azure Functions Core Tools when a local function project exists, and update app settings. Microsoft Learn's Azure CLI samples emphasize Flex Consumption as the recommended new serverless hosting plan, but the common CLI automation surface still includes classic Consumption and zip deployment flows that agents will regularly encounter.
triggers: deploy a function app zip to azure
publish a function app with core tools
provision a function app from cli
body:
# Azure Functions

Function Apps combine a hosting plan, runtime configuration, and a required storage account. The Azure CLI is best at creating and configuring the Azure resources; Azure Functions Core Tools is best at creating local code projects and publishing source trees directly.

~~~bash
# Create the backing Function App on Linux Consumption
az functionapp create \
  --resource-group rg-dojo-func \
  --consumption-plan-location eastus \
  --runtime python \
  --runtime-version 3.11 \
  --functions-version 4 \
  --name dojo-func-prod \
  --storage-account dojobuildstore \
  --os-type Linux

# Deploy a built zip package
az functionapp deployment source config-zip \
  --resource-group rg-dojo-func \
  --name dojo-func-prod \
  --src ./dist/functionapp.zip

# Publish directly from a local function project
func azure functionapp publish dojo-func-prod

# Update app settings
az functionapp config appsettings set \
  --resource-group rg-dojo-func \
  --name dojo-func-prod \
  --settings FUNCTIONS_WORKER_RUNTIME=python SCM_DO_BUILD_DURING_DEPLOYMENT=true
~~~

Use zip deploy when a CI pipeline has already produced an artifact. Use Core Tools publishing when the agent holds the function project directory and wants the Functions toolchain to package it. Either way, keep the storage account available and continue into [[azure/monitor]] for post-deploy validation.

This skill depends on [[azure/storage]] because every Function App needs a storage account, and it pairs well with [[azure/key-vault]] once secrets or connection strings must move out of plain app settings.

sections.title: Provision the Function App
sections.body: `az functionapp create` is the infrastructure step. It needs a resource group, region, runtime, function app name, and storage account. The agent should create or verify the storage account first, then create the Function App with the matching runtime and Functions version.

sections.title: Zip deployment
sections.body: `az functionapp deployment source config-zip --src ...` pushes a prepared zip package through Kudu. By default, Kudu assumes the zip does not need build actions unless `.deployment` or relevant app settings enable them. This is the cleanest path when build and deploy are separate pipeline stages.

sections.title: Core Tools publishing
sections.body: Azure's CLI samples explicitly note that Azure Functions Core Tools is required to create local function code projects and publish code projects from the command line. Use `func azure functionapp publish <name>` when the source tree is local and the Functions toolchain should own packaging.

sections.title: Settings and runtime behavior
sections.body: `az functionapp config appsettings set` updates application settings such as runtime flags, build behavior, and connection strings. Keep durable secrets in [[azure/key-vault]] when possible, and use managed identity or indirection patterns instead of hard-coding secrets directly into deployment manifests.

links.context: Authenticate and select the correct subscription before provisioning serverless resources
Create or verify the workload group first
Provision the required storage account before the Function App
Store secrets outside raw app settings when possible
Inspect deployment events and runtime health after publish

related.note: Function Apps require a storage account for runtime state and triggers
AWS Lambda is the comparable serverless surface in another cloud ecosystem
Google Cloud Functions is another serverless execution target

scripts.name: Manage Function App
scripts.description: Create a Function App, zip deploy an artifact, publish with Core Tools, or update app settings

AZURE_FUNCTIONAPP_ACTION.description: One of app-create, deploy-zip, publish-core-tools, settings-set, or show

AZURE_FUNCTIONS_RUNTIME_VERSION.description: Runtime version such as 3.11 for Python

AZURE_FUNCTIONAPP_SRC_PATH.description: Local source directory for Core Tools publishing

AZURE_FUNCTIONAPP_BUILD_REMOTE.description: Set to true to pass --build-remote during zip deploy

AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID.description: Subscription ID or name to activate before the command

runtime.description: Runtime such as python or node

sourcePath.description: Local Function project directory for Core Tools publish

kind.description: Azure resource kind for the Function App

depends.reason: Provisioning and deployment require an authenticated Azure principal
Function Apps deploy into a known resource group
Function Apps require a storage account
The executable wrapper is a Bash script
